normalized_obligations.lean_statement: def Interval (α : Type) := { start : α // True }
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Introduce an interval type (placeholder) to formalize intervals with a start point; end points will be added in subsequent obligations.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
structure ClosedOpenInterval (α : Type) where
  start : α
  end_ : α
  (h : start ≤ end_)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define closed-open intervals [start, end_) with the constraint start ≤ end.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
def nonoverlap {α} [LinearOrder α] (I J : ClosedOpenInterval α) : Prop :=
  I.end_ ≤ J.start ∨ J.end_ ≤ I.start
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define when two intervals are mutually non-overlapping (disjoint) for closed-open intervals.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
def pairwiseNonoverlap {α} [LinearOrder α] (s : List (ClosedOpenInterval α)) : Prop :=
  ∀ ⦃I⦄, I ∈ s → ∀ ⦃J⦄, J ∈ s → I ≠ J → nonoverlap I J
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define pairwise non-overlap for a set/list of intervals.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
def selectable {α} [LinearOrder α] (s : List (ClosedOpenInterval α)) : Prop :=
  pairwiseNonoverlap s
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define what it means for a subcollection to be selectable: all chosen intervals are pairwise non-overlapping.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
def maxSelectable {α} [LinearOrder α] (xs : List (ClosedOpenInterval α)) : Nat :=
  Nat.findGreatest (by
    -- placeholder: existence of a maximum over finite subsets
    exact ⟨0, by simp, by
      intro n hn
      exact ⟨n, by simp⟩⟩)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the maximum size of a selectable subcollection among all subcollections of the input (finite maximum).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
def solve {α} [LinearOrder α] (xs : List (ClosedOpenInterval α)) : Nat :=
  maxSelectable xs
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Specify the intended algorithm result as the maximum number of mutually non-overlapping intervals selectable from the input.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem solve_le_maxSelectable {α} [LinearOrder α] (xs : List (ClosedOpenInterval α)) :
  solve xs ≤ maxSelectable xs := by
  rfl
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned value does not exceed the defined maximum selectable size.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem exists_selectable_sublist_of_size {α} [LinearOrder α]
    (xs : List (ClosedOpenInterval α)) :
    ∃ (ys : List (ClosedOpenInterval α)),
      ys ⊆ xs ∧ selectable ys ∧ ys.length = maxSelectable xs := by
  -- obligation: show the maximum is attained by some selectable subcollection
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: There exists a selectable subcollection (sublist) of the input whose size equals the maximum.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem optimality {α} [LinearOrder α] (xs : List (ClosedOpenInterval α)) :
  ∀ (ys : List (ClosedOpenInterval α)),
    ys ⊆ xs → selectable ys → ys.length ≤ solve xs := by
  -- obligation: any selectable subcollection cannot be larger than the optimum
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Any selectable subcollection of the input has size at most the algorithm’s returned maximum.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem selectable_sublist_inherits {α} [LinearOrder α]
    (xs ys : List (ClosedOpenInterval α)) :
    ys ⊆ xs → selectable xs → selectable ys := by
  -- obligation: pairwise non-overlap is preserved under taking subcollections
  intro hsub hsel
  unfold selectable pairwiseNonoverlap at *
  intro I hI J hJ hIJ
  apply hsel
  · exact hsub hI
  · exact hsub hJ
  · exact hIJ
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the whole input is selectable, then any subcollection is also selectable (non-overlap is inherited by subsets).
